{{Quote|There's still enough explosive in this fuse to take out half of Mos Eisley!|Anonymous [[Aeropteryx]]|<ref name="The Mandalorian Armor">''[[The Mandalorian Armor]]''</ref>}}
'''Atmospheric phase-change detonators''' were detonation devices for the ''Imperial''-class [[M-12]] sweep bomb in the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]]. The detonator remained a potent explosive device even after being removed from its bomb host.

The detonator was spherical, with two off-center cylindrical protrusions and only three tiny red LEDs on its thumbnail-sized readout showing whether it was active.

Manufactured by a subcontractor on [[Kuat/Legends|Kuat]] through the [[Kuat Drive Yards/Legends|Kuat Drive Yards]], each production ran into numbers of 250,000. Kuat Drive Yards reserved serial designations below twelve million for testing on their heavy cruisers and destroyers' munitions-storage chambers.

By [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], these detonators had been discontinued due to a near ten percent failure rate in the field. Only a sentient with experience could spot a dud. The [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Empire]] replaced them with an integrated gravity-wave system encased inside the main explosive.

Lone detonators found their way into the hands of others, such as [[Dengar/Legends|Dengar]].

==Appearances==
*''[[The Mandalorian Armor]]''
